dsl_rule.translation_notes: Re-entry triggered by new directional signal: price closes above 20-period Donchian upper (new high equivalent) AND ADX confirms trending market. Exit on pivot support breakdown or original 1% stop-loss. Signal applies to both LONG and SHORT contexts depending on direction of initial stopped-out position. Donchian upper approximates 'new high' requirement; ADX >25 confirms trending regime alignment per target_regime specification.

backtest_rule.reason: Re-entry triggered by new directional signal: price closes above 20-period Donchian upper (new high equivalent) AND ADX confirms trending market. Exit on pivot support breakdown or original 1% stop-loss. Signal applies to both LONG and SHORT contexts depending on direction of initial stopped-out position. Donchian upper approximates 'new high' requirement; ADX >25 confirms trending regime alignment per target_regime specification.

source_rule_text: A trader should re-enter a stopped-out position only after the market generates a new directional signal (such as a new high) rather than attempting to re-enter on any reversal, to avoid whipsaw and ensure alignment with the system's intent.
